transformation_type: aldehyde or ketone to α-amino nitrile
mechanism_type: imine/iminium formation followed by cyanide addition
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde or ketone + amine + cyanide source
common_conditions: amine condensation followed by cyanide addition, often acidic or Lewis acidic
description_short: Classical and asymmetric cyanide addition to imines generated from carbonyl compounds and amines to access α-amino nitriles and amino acid precursors.

transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed coupling of organoborons with electrophiles
mechanism_type: oxidative addition / base-promoted transmetallation / reductive elimination
description_short: Palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling between organoboron nucleophiles and organic halides or related electrophiles under basic conditions.

transformation_type: alcohol oxidation to aldehydes or ketones
reactant_pattern_text: primary or secondary alcohol + activated DMSO reagent
common_conditions: low temperature, activated DMSO followed by base
description_short: Low-temperature oxidation of alcohols with DMSO activated by oxalyl chloride or related reagents to give aldehydes or ketones.

common_conditions: chromium(II) chloride-mediated olefination, often with CHI3/CHBr3
description_short: Chromium(II)-mediated olefination of aldehydes to stereodefined alkenyl halides, generally favoring the E isomer.

common_conditions: titanocene methylidene generation with Lewis base or organoaluminum additive
description_short: Titanocene methylidene-mediated conversion of carbonyl compounds to methylene derivatives, including alkenes and enol ethers.
